danger_signals.visual_form: Monster/villain sprites with distinctive animation and movement patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat engagement or avoidance
Spatial danger requiring precise movement and timing to avoid damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Enlarged enemy sprite with dramatic entrance animation
danger_signals.meaning: High-stakes combat scenario with increased difficulty and resource drain

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprites (likely health/energy restores based on genre conventions)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability for sustained combat capability
Safe progression through world; advancement toward objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character transformed in colored suit armor
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced combat capability and protection status

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multiple (Red, Blue, Yellow, Pink, Black - based on TV show team roster)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ranger suit morphology; iconic armor design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Environments featuring Angel Grove locations (implied from source material)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active monster attack on Angel Grove
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage populated with enemy sprites; environmental destruction implied through platforming hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and attack with varying AI patterns; combat encounters escalate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ranger sprite may show damage or visual degradation; reduced visual effects during attacks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player movement may slow; attack effectiveness diminishes visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dramatic visual transformation animation; character transitions from unmorphed to morphed suit state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporary invulnerability period; enhanced movement and attack speed post-transformation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage exit becomes accessible; visual fade-out or transition animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies despawn; player gains free movement to level exit

environmental_storytelling.example: Each level represents a different Angel Grove location under attack, establishing the crisis scope through environment selection
Monster and Putty Patroller distribution suggests invasion progression; density increases toward boss encounter
Platforming challenges and environmental obstacles suggest environmental corruption or destruction from monster activity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard movement speed; basic attack capability; vulnerable to damage
Enhanced movement capabilities; powerful special attacks; increased defensive resilience
Predictable movement patterns; basic melee attacks; low individual threat
Aggressive pursuit; coordinated multi-unit attacks; higher frequency of strikes
Complex attack patterns; stage-specific mechanics; multi-phase engagement requiring sustained strategy
Destruction sequence; visual demise animation; environmental state resets

notes: Source material (TCRF) primarily documents technical/development content (sound test, source code, regional differences) rather than manifestation layer data. Visual communication patterns inferred from standard platformer conventions and Power Rangers IP conventions. No explicit documentation of faction symbols, color coding, or state-change visuals in provided source. Game communicates danger through enemy and hazard sprite presence; safety through morphed state transformation and item collection. The SNES technical limitations inform visual language—sprite-based enemies, simple animation cycles, distinct character states differentiated through visual transformation rather than subtle shader/effect changes.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Mighty Morphin Power Rangers (SNES) - Technical/developmental data only; visual manifestation data inferred from platformer genre standards and TV show source material
